The primary biochemical pathways involved in the repair of UV-induced DNA damage are nucleotide excision repair (NER) and base excision repair (BER). DNA damage caused by UV is primarily repaired by NER, which recognizes bulky DNA adducts, such as pyrimidine dimers. BER is mainly involved in the repair of small, non-helix-distorting base lesions

69a4cc0a85870e396d000008.raw:
requiring careful patient monitoring and management.

Snippet 2: Kymriah is a CD19-directed chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy. It is derived from autologous T cells collected from the patient. The treatment is used for patients with relapsed or refractory B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL). It is also used for patients with certain types of large B-cell lymphoma. Known side effects include cytokine release syndrome (CRS), neurotoxicity, hypogammaglobulinemia, and tumor lysis syndrome, among others.

Snippet 3: Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) is a common side effect of CAR T-cell therapy, which can occur within days of treatment. Symptoms include fever, chills, hypotension, tachycardia, and sometimes respiratory distress. Severe cases may require intensive care. CAR T-cell-related encephalopathy syndrome (CRES) is another potential complication, characterized by confusion, aphasia, and delirium, which typically resolves within a few weeks.

Snippet 4: Hypogammaglobulinemia is a side effect that can occur after Kymriah therapy, leading to an increased risk of infections. Patients are advised to receive immunoglobulin replacement therapy to mitigate this risk. Other side effects include fever, fatigue, and gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea and diarrhea.

Snippet 5: Tumor lysis syndrome (TLS) is another side effect associated with Kymriah. It occurs when a large number of tumor cells are rapidly killed, leading to the release of intracellular contents into the bloodstream. This can result in electrolyte imbalances, kidney damage, and acute kidney injury. Management includes aggressive hydration, alkalinization of urine, and monitoring of renal function.
